Description: A unique short identifier (e.g., AuthorYear) for each study.
The authors of the publication.
The year the study was published.
The name of the journal where the study was published.
The Digital Object Identifier for the article.
The total number of participants with Parkinson's Disease (PD).
The minimum age of the PD participants.
The maximum age of the PD participants.
The average age of the PD participants.
The standard deviation of the PD participants' age.
The average UPDRS score for PD patients in their 'ON' medication state.
The standard deviation of the 'ON' medication UPDRS score.
The median UPDRS score for PD patients in their 'ON' medication state.
The interquartile range of the 'ON' medication UPDRS score.
The average UPDRS score for PD patients in their 'OFF' medication state.
The standard deviation of the 'OFF' medication UPDRS score.
The median UPDRS score for PD patients in their 'OFF' medication state.
The interquartile range of the 'OFF' medication UPDRS score.
The average Hoehn & Yahr stage, indicating PD severity.
The standard deviation of the Hoehn & Yahr stage.
The median Hoehn & Yahr stage.
The interquartile range of the Hoehn & Yahr stage.
The minimum Hoehn & Yahr stage.
The maximum Hoehn & Yahr stage.
The medication status of PD patients at the time of the fMRI scan (e.g., ON, OFF).
The total number of Healthy Control (HC) participants.
The average age of the HC participants.
The standard deviation of the HC participants' age.
The overall design of the study (e.g., Cross-sectional, Longitudinal).
The main topic of the investigation (e.g., Motor Symptoms, Cognitive Impairment).
A detailed sub-category of the primary focus (e.g., Tremor, FOG, Apathy).
The origin of the dataset (e.g., Public, Private/In-house).
The specific database or institution that provided the data (e.g., PPMI).
The type of fMRI experiment performed (e.g., Resting-state, Task-based).
A brief description of the task, if one was used.
Repetition Time (the time to acquire one fMRI volume) in milliseconds.
Echo Time (the time to measure the fMRI signal) in milliseconds.
The total number of brain volumes collected during the scan.
The total duration of the fMRI scan in minutes.
The dimensions of the fMRI voxels (e.g., 3x3x3 mm).
The magnetic field strength of the MRI scanner in Tesla (T).
The number of head motion parameters regressed (e.g., 6p, 24p).
Whether White Matter & Cerebrospinal Fluid signals were regressed out (Yes/No).
Whether CompCor denoising was used (Yes/No).
Whether ICA-AROMA denoising was used (Yes/No).
Whether signal detrending was applied (Yes/No).
Whether signal despiking was applied (Yes.No).
Whether motion scrubbing (frame censoring) was applied (Yes/No).
The Mean Framewise Displacement threshold (in mm) for subject exclusion.
The Maximum Framewise Displacement threshold (in mm) for subject exclusion.
The Maximum Translation threshold (in mm) for subject exclusion.
The Maximum Rotation threshold (in degrees) for subject exclusion.
The threshold for the percentage of outlier frames for subject exclusion.
Any other or qualitative criteria used for motion exclusion.
Whether Global Signal Regression was performed (Yes/No).
The kernel size (FWHM in mm) used for spatial smoothing.
The type of temporal filtering applied (e.g., Band-pass, Low-pass).
The low-frequency cutoff (in Hz) for the temporal filter.
The high-frequency cutoff (in Hz) for the temporal filter.
The type of a second temporal filtering step, if applied.
The low-frequency cutoff for a second filtering step.
The high-frequency cutoff for a second filtering step.
The level of analysis (e.g., Areas, Networks, Whole Brain).
The number of networks used in a network-based analysis.
The number of areas or ROIs defined by the parcellation.
A list of the specific brain networks or areas investigated.
The brain atlas or method used to define regions (e.g., AAL, Schaefer).
The computational method used to measure dFC (e.g., Sliding window, d-CAPs).
The length of the sliding window (in seconds) for dFC analysis.
The step size (in seconds) the sliding window moved by.
The algorithm used to identify brain states (e.g., k-means).
The final number of dFC states identified.
The method used to select the optimal number of states (e.g., Silhouette index).
The dynamic properties extracted from the states (e.g., Dwell time, Fraction time).
Any graph theory metrics computed (e.g., Global efficiency, Modularity).
Information on whether the raw data is publicly available.
Information on whether the analysis code is publicly available.
A qualitative description of the identified brain states.
Specific findings related to highly integrated brain states.
Specific findings related to highly segregated brain states.
The study's primary conclusion about integration vs. segregation.
Specific findings related to the transitions between brain states.
The study's primary conclusion about the patterns of state transitions.
The key finding or proposed biomarker summarized from the study.
The study's limitations as reported by the authors.
